round1: Round 1 is wrapped, and some of you clearly spent more time on your fantasy football lineups. Marc, two correct picks? Impressive dedication to being wrong. And to Chris and Steph, picking Ottawa to beat Pittsburgh was a bold strategy that paid off exactly zero times.
round2: Round 2 was a bloodbath for most, as only Nickall had the foresight to bet against Montreal. While Marc, Chris, and Jaclyn managed to salvage some dignity with a perfect Penguins pick, Jaclyn and Nickall's inexplicable faith in Colorado was spectacularly wrong. Better luck in the next round, if you still have any picks left.
round3: Round 3 is done. Everyone correctly picked Pittsburgh and Detroit to advance, but nobody got the game counts right. Well, almost everyone. Derrick, you managed to pick two losers and miss every single game count; truly a masterclass in futility.
round4: And that's Round 4 wrapped up. Chris, congratulations on being the only person to actually pick Detroit. The rest of you, a magnificent display of collective delusion, picking Pittsburgh like the Red Wings weren't even on the ice.
overall: Alright, the 2008 NHL playoffs are done, and the Detroit Red Wings rightfully took home the Cup, proving that sometimes the best team actually wins. In our family pool, Chrissy put on an absolute clinic with 47 points, dominating the competition and making a mockery of everyone else's picks, with Jaclyn's respectable 44 points barely keeping her in the same zip code. Nickall and Marc managed to finish in the middle, but the real standout performance in futility belongs to Stephanie, who somehow picked nine teams and only got two series correct, tying Derrick for last place with a pathetic 20 points. Congratulations Chrissy, you've earned your bragging rights; as for the rest of you, maybe try watching a game or two next year.
